data.title: FDA accepts Teva's NDA for TEV-'749, a monthly treatment for schizophrenia in adults, addressing treatment gap.
data.description: In Parsippany, N.J., Tel Aviv, Israel, and Paris, a notable pharmaceutical collaboration between Teva Pharmaceuticals, a U.S. affiliate of Teva Pharmaceutical Industries Ltd. (NYSE and TASE: TEVA), and Medincell (Euronext: MEDCL) was marked on Feb. 20, 2026. They disclosed that the New Drug Application (NDA) for olanzapine extended-release injectable suspension (TEV-'749) has been officially accepted by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA). This novel drug is aimed at treating schizophrenia in adults, providing a potential solution to the challenge of maintaining long-term stability and treatment adherence for individuals with schizophrenia. This development signifies a significant step towards bridging the existing treatment gap for patients dealing with this mental health condition.

data.title: Board chair John Demyan retires after 30 years; Jay Baldwin appointed new chair of Glen Burnie Bancorp. Tommy Clocker also retiring May 14.
data.description: In Glen Burnie, Maryland, on February 20, 2026, Glen Burnie Bancorp ("Company") (OTCQX: GLBZ), the bank holding company for The Bank of Glen Burnie ("Bank"), revealed that Jay Baldwin, a current board member and notable local business figure, has taken over as Chair of the Board of Directors with immediate effect. Baldwin assumes this position following the retirement announcement of John "Jack" E. Demyan, who had been serving as Chair since 1996. Demyan, who will continue as a board member, was honored with the title Chairman Emeritus for his years of service. Moreover, another long-standing director, Thomas "Tommy" Clocker, disclosed his plan to retire on May 14, 2026, having been a director since 1995.

data.title: Barco Products introduces new BABA-compliant traffic safety solutions with recycled rubber products.
data.description: Batavia, Illinois - Barco Products, a prominent provider of premium site furnishings and traffic safety items, has unveiled a range of recycled rubber speed bumps and wheel stops compliant with the Buy America, Build America (BABA) standards. The latest collection comprises the Standard Recycled Rubber Speed Bump Kit, Speed Bump Sections, End Caps, and Wheel Stops, all crafted for long-lasting performance. These items are constructed from 100% recycled rubber, meeting the BABA requirements for sustainable sourcing and manufacturing.

data.title: New Xfinity store opens in South Bend at 4615 Miami Street; grand opening on Feb 19 with Mayor and local officials.
data.description: Comcast unveiled a new Xfinity Store in South Bend, Indiana, located at 4615 Miami Street by the Erskine Village Shopping Center. The grand opening took place on February 19, attended by Mayor James Mueller, South Bend Regional Chamber of Commerce members, and various city representatives. This innovative storefront boasts an interactive layout, providing customers with a unique experience.

data.title: Inventor from Sudbury, ON creates personalized headstone called DOOCH DIGITAL to honor departed loved ones.
data.description: Inventor from Sudbury, Ontario, Canada, introduces the DOOCH DIGITAL headstone to personalize and honor departed loved ones. The device aims to remind friends and family with a unique touch. The product hopes to bring comfort by incorporating digital elements into traditional memorials.

data.title: Stocks rise as Nasdaq gains on news of Trump's global tariff despite Supreme Court ruling against him.
data.description: Today, following the Supreme Court's decision against President Trump's global tariffs, the stock market saw an increase. The Nasdaq experienced gains as Trump announced a global tariff despite the court's ruling. This information was provided in a live coverage by Investor's Business Daily.

data.title: Taylor Mason joins Stateside as Vice President, supporting clients in state and local government relations.
data.description: On February 20, 2026, Stateside Associates, a leading state and local government relations firm, revealed the appointment of Taylor Mason as Vice President. Taylor will play a crucial role in assisting clients with the intricate challenges of government affairs. This news signifies a major move for Stateside Associates in bolstering their team with key talent.

data.title: Veterans United employees donated $14.3 million in 2025 through Veterans United Foundation to help nationwide communities.
data.description: In February 2026, in Columbia, Missouri, the Veterans United Foundation, supported by employees of Veterans United Home Loans and its affiliated companies, made a significant contribution. Throughout 2025, the foundation donated $14,349,752 to various charitable organizations, community projects, and individuals in need nationwide. This substantial figure represents the generous efforts and philanthropic endeavors of the foundation and its contributors.
